title: BluMind Benchmark — Evaluation schema v1.0
description: Structured scoring produced by a human reviewer on a single (case, subject, run) response. Source of truth lives under scoring/v1.0/<subject_id>/<case_id>__<run_id>__eval-<reviewer_id>.json. Philosophy: the reviewer declares inputs (per-criterion scores, automatic fails triggered, correctness for calibration, notes). Derived outputs (raw_score 0-12, clipped_score after rubric_ceiling, Pass/Conditional/Fail classification, ECE/Brier contributions) are NOT stored here — they are computed downstream by scripts/aggregate_scoring.py and scripts/compute_metrics.py from this file plus the gold, and written to results/. This separation guarantees a single source of truth per computed value.

evaluation_id.description: Unique identifier for this scoring. Recommended convention: eval-<case_id>-<run_id>-<reviewer_id> (e.g. 'eval-RO-FOUL-001-run-2026-04-22-001-R01'). Must be unique across scoring/ for the same benchmark_version.

benchmark_version.description: Benchmark version this scoring is bound to (e.g. 'v1.0'). Must match the response's benchmark_version; CI enforces the cross-file coherence.

case_id.description: Case identifier of the response being scored.

subject_version.description: Specific version or snapshot of the evaluated subject (e.g. 'gpt-5-2026-03-15').

run_id.description: Run identifier of the response being scored.

response_ref.description: Denormalized pointer to the scored response. Redundant with the response file but kept explicit to enable independent queries against scoring/ without joining responses/.

reviewer_id.description: Anonymized reviewer identifier (e.g. 'R01', 'R02'). v1.0 accepts any non-empty string; v2.0 will bind this to a reviewer registry (see README 'Scope explicitly deferred to v2.0').

scoring_timestamp_utc.description: Moment at which the reviewer finalized the scoring, in UTC ISO 8601.

criterion_scores.description: One score 0-2 per rubric criterion, each with mandatory justification. Field names mirror the response schema fields they evaluate (see rubric's 'Design rule' mapping table).

type.description: Whether this fail is defined in the generic rubric or in the gold of the specific case.

reference.description: For type='generic': the fail number from the rubric's 'Generic automatic fails' section (e.g. '1', '2', '5'). For type='case_specific': the exact 'description' string from the gold's case_specific_automatic_fails entry that was triggered.

severity.description: Severity applied. Must match either the generic rubric default or the gold's declared severity. The gold may escalate (but never downgrade) the generic severity; severity stored here is the effective one after that override.

justification.description: Reviewer's technical justification for triggering this fail on this specific response.

automatic_fails_triggered.description: Zero or more automatic fails triggered by the response. Empty array is valid (response free of fails). Coherence with the gold (for case_specific) and the rubric (for generic) is enforced by scripts/validate_evaluation.py, not by this schema.

correctness.description: Main hypothesis correctness against the gold, for longitudinal calibration only: 1 = correct, 0.5 = partially correct, 0 = incorrect. This feeds ECE and Brier score computation paired with the response's 'confidence' field. Per-case Pass/Conditional/Fail classification does NOT use this value (it uses criterion_scores + automatic_fails_triggered + gold's rubric_floor/ceiling).

reviewer_notes.description: Free-form notes from the reviewer: context, doubts, cross-references to similar cases, concerns about the rubric itself. May be empty string. Not parsed by any downstream pipeline; preserved for future committee review and rubric refinement.

score.description: 0, 1, or 2 per the rubric's scoring criteria table.

justification.description: Reviewer's technical justification for the score. Required even for a score of 2 (to enable later audit and inter-reviewer comparison).

justification_long.description: PREMIUM / EXPLANATIONS-tier field — OPTIONAL. Long-form expert critique in rich prose (3-6 sentences) explaining the engineering reasoning behind the score: what the model got right, what an expert would add or correct, and the technical context (thresholds, mechanisms, trade-offs, non-obvious anchors) needed for an auto-judge to learn from this case. This field is the per-criterion training material for BluMind's commercial evaluator dataset (`explanations.jsonl`). It is INTENTIONALLY OMITTED from the public benchmark export by `scripts/export_public.py` — only the schema is public, the content is not. Reviewers fill it for top-3 and bottom-3 responses per case (the highest-signal items for evaluator training); leaving it absent is valid and means the entry is not part of the commercial export.
